core_direction: Use specialist agents for research, design, build, review, and trend analysis
Treat authn, authz, approval, policy, and audit as first-class concerns
Favor small safe increments over broad speculative refactors
must_preserve: Authenticated actor resolution on protected approval endpoints
Server-side actor authority over client-provided actor fields
Audit visibility for authentication, authorization, override, and approval decisions
Backward-compatible behavior unless a deprecation is explicitly documented
do_not_do: Do not merge Intake and Orchestrator responsibilities
Do not let provider-specific logic leak into core orchestration
Do not weaken strict_mode or policy enforcement silently
Do not add new infrastructure or dependencies without justification
Do not change schema or migration behavior without review
Do not replace tested behavior with speculative redesign
continue_if: Tests for the touched area pass
The change fits the current roadmap phase
No auth/policy/security boundary changes are required
pause_if: Verification fails and root cause is not yet isolated
The next step is unclear from roadmap and codebase
The change risks regression in approval, auth, or audit flows
review_if: Auth, approval, or policy behavior must change
The code suggests a larger architectural mismatch
